solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isSubsequence(String s, String t) {
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while (i < s.length() && j < t.length()) {
            if (s.charAt(i) == t.charAt(j)) {
                i++;
            }
            j++;
        }
        return i == s.length();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a two-pointer approach to check if all characters of string `s` appear in `t` in order. It iterates through `t` and matches characters with `s`. If all characters in `s` are matched, it is a subsequence. The key strength is its linear time and constant space complexity, making it efficient for large inputs. The weakness is that it is not optimized for the follow-up scenario with a large number of queries.
